meta.title: MS Club Mini Hackathon 2026 — Production Knowledge Base
meta.source: Official Rules & Regulations document + Supplementary information
meta.description: RAG-optimized, semantically rich knowledge base covering every aspect of Mini Hackathon 2026. Designed for high retrieval accuracy with dense keyword coverage, alternative phrasings, and comprehensive answers.

greeting: Hi there! 👋 I'm the Mini Hackathon 2026 AI assistant. Ask me anything eligibility, registration, rounds, tracks, prizes, judging, or anything else about the competition!
fallback: Hmm, I'm not sure about that one! Try rephrasing your question, or ask about topics like registration, eligibility, team formation, the competition rounds, tracks, prizes, or judging criteria. You can also reach us at info@msclubsliit.org for anything not covered here!

faqs.question: What kind of questions can I ask you?
faqs.context: User is asking about the chatbot's capabilities or what topics they can ask about
keywords: what kind of questions can i ask
in wich kind ow question ask for this hackathon
faqs.answer:
I'm the official Mini Hackathon 2026 AI Assistant! You can ask me anything about the competition, such as:

📅 **Registration & Eligibility**: How to sign up, who can join, and deadlines.
🏅 **Competition Rounds**: What to submit, the format, and schedules for Round 1, Round 2, and the Grand Finale.
🎯 **Tracks & Topics**: The 6 competition tracks (AI, IoT, Web3, Sustainability, Society, Emerging Tech) and project ideas.
🏆 **Prizes & Judging**: The cash prizes, certificates, and how your project will be evaluated.

Just type your question below! (e.g., 'How do I register?' or 'What are the prizes?')

faqs.question: Greeting or saying hello to the chatbot
faqs.context: User is initiating a conversation or greeting the bot
faqs.answer:
Hello! 👋 Welcome to Mini Hackathon 2026. I'm your AI assistant and I'm here to help you with everything about the competition.

You can ask me about:
• Registration & eligibility
• Competition rounds and what to submit
• Tracks and topics
• Prizes and judging criteria
• Rules and policies

What would you like to know?

faqs.question: User saying thank you or expressing gratitude
faqs.context: User is thanking the bot or expressing satisfaction
faqs.answer: You're very welcome! 😊 I'm always here if you have more questions. Good luck with your Mini Hackathon journey!

faqs.question: What is the Mini Hackathon 2026? Overview and purpose of the competition.
faqs.context: User wants to understand what this event is, its mission, and what it offers participants
faqs.answer:
Mini Hackathon 2026 (MH26) is a prestigious, skill-based software competition organized by the MS Club of SLIIT — Sri Lanka's leading undergraduate tech club.

🎯 Our Mission:
To discover and celebrate the most innovative, accessible, and ground-breaking software solutions built by SLIIT undergrads that empower youth and solve real-world problems.

✨ What Makes It Special:
• Compete across 6 cutting-edge technology tracks
• Get mentored by industry experts (in Round 2)
• Build real products and gain hands-on experience
• Win exciting cash prizes and recognition
• Network with tech leaders and fellow innovators
• Earn certificates and exclusive MS Club swag

The competition runs in 3 progressive rounds — from virtual proposal submission all the way to a live, on-the-spot physical hackathon at SLIIT Malabe campus!

faqs.question: Who organizes the Mini Hackathon? Who is the MS Club of SLIIT?
faqs.context: User wants to know who is running this event and who the organizing body is
faqs.answer:
Mini Hackathon 2026 is organized by the MS Club of SLIIT — the official Microsoft Student Club at the Sri Lanka Institute of Information Technology (SLIIT).

🏢 About the MS Club of SLIIT:
• Official Microsoft-recognized student community at SLIIT
• One of Sri Lanka's most active undergraduate tech clubs
• Hosts events, workshops, and competitions year-round
• Connected to a large network of industry professionals and mentors

📌 In the official rules:
• 'We', 'Our', 'Us', and 'Sponsor' always refer to the MS Club of SLIIT
• 'You' and 'Yourself' always refer to the eligible participant

🌐 Website: minihackathon.msclubsliit.org
📧 Email: info@msclubsliit.org

faqs.question: Who is eligible to participate in the Mini Hackathon? Requirements to join.
faqs.context: User wants to know if they qualify, what the age requirements are, what year students are allowed, and what conditions must be met
faqs.answer:
To be eligible for Mini Hackathon 2026, you must meet ALL of the following:

✅ Academic Status:
• Be an actively enrolled SLIIT undergraduate student
• Must be in 1st Year, 2nd Year, OR 3rd Year (1st Semester only)
• Note: 3rd Year 2nd Semester students and above are NOT eligible

✅ Age Requirement:
• Must be at least 16 years old as of 2 September 2025

✅ Conduct Requirements:
• No paid assistance for your submission
• No use of stolen or unauthorized intellectual property (IP)
• Must adhere to all official rules and code of conduct

❌ NOT Eligible:
• Postgraduate (Masters/PhD) students
• Students not enrolled at SLIIT
• Students in 3rd Year 2nd Semester or beyond
• Anyone under 16 years old

If you're unsure, reach out to info@msclubsliit.org for clarification!

faqs.question: Can I participate again if I competed in a previous Mini Hackathon?
faqs.context: User has participated in a past Mini Hackathon edition and wants to know if they can re-enter
faqs.answer:
Yes! If you've competed in a previous Mini Hackathon, you are absolutely welcome to enter again — provided you still meet the eligibility requirements.

⚠️ Important Rule:
Your new submission MUST be a substantially new, unique, and different project. You cannot reuse or resubmit an old project from a previous edition.

So feel free to come back with a fresh, innovative idea! 🚀

faqs.question: How many members can be on a team? Team formation and size rules.
faqs.context: User wants to know the minimum and maximum team size, and how to form a team
keywords: can i join without a team
faqs.answer:
Teams must have exactly 3 or 4 members. No more, no less.

👥 Team Formation Rules:
• Minimum: 3 members
• Maximum: 4 members
• Solo or 2-person entries are NOT allowed
• All members must individually meet the eligibility requirements

💡 Pro Tip:
We highly recommend mixing students from different degree programs, faculties, or areas of expertise (e.g., engineering + design + business). Diverse teams tend to produce more well-rounded and innovative solutions!

📌 Note: Once registered, you cannot add or remove team members, so choose wisely before registering.

faqs.question: How do I register my team? Step-by-step registration process.
faqs.context: User wants to know the exact steps to sign up, where to register, what to fill in, and who does the registration
faqs.answer:
Here's the complete step-by-step guide to register your team:

📋 Step 1: Form Your Team
Recruit 2 or 3 other eligible SLIIT undergrads (total team: 3–4 members).

💡 Step 2: Choose a Track
Select a unique project idea from one of the 6 competition tracks.

👤 Step 3: Designate a Team Representative
One team member should be appointed to handle the registration on behalf of the entire team.

🌐 Step 4: Visit the Official Website
Go to minihackathon.msclubsliit.org and click 'Register Now'.

📝 Step 5: Fill In the Form
Complete all required team and individual member details accurately.

✅ Step 6: Submit Before the Deadline
Your registration must be submitted before 30 September 2025.

⚠️ Remember: Only one registration per team. Only your first submission counts.

faqs.question: When does registration open and close? What are the registration dates and deadlines?
faqs.context: User wants to know the registration window — when they can start registering and the final deadline
faqs.answer:
📅 Registration Period for Mini Hackathon 2026:

• Opens: 00:01 AM on 1 September 2025
• Closes: 30 September 2025 (end of day)

⚠️ Important Notes:
• Late registrations will NOT be accepted under any circumstances
• Register early to give yourself time to prepare a strong submission
• All team members must meet eligibility criteria at the time of registration

🌐 Register at: minihackathon.msclubsliit.org

faqs.question: Can a team submit more than one entry or registration?
faqs.context: User wants to know if they can submit multiple entries, change their submission, or have multiple attempts
faqs.answer:
No — each team may submit only ONE entry per round.

⚠️ Critical Rule:
• Only your FIRST submission will be counted
• Any subsequent or duplicate submissions will be automatically discarded
• Choose your track and project idea carefully before submitting

If you made an error in your registration, contact info@msclubsliit.org as early as possible.

faqs.question: What is the full competition schedule and timeline? How many rounds are there?
faqs.context: User wants an overview of all rounds, the schedule, format (virtual vs physical), and key dates
faqs.answer:
Mini Hackathon 2026 is structured across 3 progressive rounds:

🟡 Round 1 — Virtual Screening (2–30 September 2025)
• Submit a Project Proposal + a 3-minute Video Presentation
• Evaluated by Microsoft Gold Ambassadors
• Top 16 teams advance to Round 2

🟠 Round 2 — Virtual Live Presentations (5–10 October 2025)
• Top 16 teams are each assigned a dedicated industry mentor
• Submit an updated proposal + a Figma/Adobe XD prototype
• Deliver a 10-minute live virtual presentation + 1-minute Q&A
• Top 10 teams advance to the Grand Finale

🔴 Round 3 — Grand Finale (Physical: 12 October 2025)
• Held at SLIIT Malabe Campus
• A surprise topic is assigned on the day
• 5-hour on-the-spot build challenge
• 10-minute final presentation
• Winners announced with prizes & certificates!

📌 Tip: Register by 30 September to participate in Round 1!

faqs.question: What do I need to submit for Round 1? Round 1 deliverables and requirements.
faqs.context: User wants to know exactly what they must prepare and submit for Round 1 of the competition
keywords: what do i need for round 1
faqs.answer:
📋 Round 1 — What You Need to Submit:

Round 1 runs from 2–30 September 2025 and is entirely virtual.

✅ Mandatory Deliverables:
1. Project Proposal (DOC, DOCX, or PDF — max 10 pages)
   • Must clearly state your chosen track
   • Outline your solution, target audience, and impact
   • Written in English; original work only
   • File size: less than 100MB

2. 3-Minute Presentation Video
   • Created using PowerPoint or Google Slides
   • Uploaded to Microsoft Streams using your SLIIT email
   • Voice and content must be your own — no AI voiceovers
   • Maximum duration: 3 minutes (strictly enforced)

🔶 Optional (but recommended!):
• A working software demo
• An Adobe XD or Figma prototype

💡 These optional items can significantly strengthen your entry!

🏆 The top 16 teams with the highest scores advance to Round 2.

faqs.question: How many teams advance from Round 1 to Round 2? Who evaluates Round 1?
faqs.context: User wants to know the selection process and how many teams move forward from Round 1
faqs.answer:
📊 Round 1 Advancement:

• All Round 1 submissions are reviewed and scored by Microsoft Gold Ambassadors
• Evaluation is based on the official marking criteria (Technology, Innovation, Concept, Feasibility, Presentation)
• The TOP 16 teams with the highest cumulative scores advance to Round 2

💡 Tips to maximize your Round 1 score:
• Clearly address all 5 judging criteria in your proposal
• Keep your video sharp, professional, and within 3 minutes
• Optionally include a prototype or demo to stand out
• Choose a problem that's genuinely impactful and feasible

faqs.question: What happens in Round 2? Round 2 deliverables, format, and requirements.
faqs.context: User wants to know what they need to prepare and do in Round 2 of the competition
faqs.answer:
📋 Round 2 — What You Need to Prepare:

Round 2 runs from 5–10 October 2025 and is fully virtual. Only the top 16 teams from Round 1 compete here.

✅ Deliverables:
1. Updated Project Proposal (revised from Round 1)
2. Adobe XD or Figma UI Prototype (mandatory in Round 2)
3. 10-Minute Live Virtual Presentation to the judges
4. 1-Minute Q&A Session with the judging panel

🎓 Mentor Support:
• Each advancing team is assigned a dedicated industry expert mentor
• Use this mentorship to refine your solution before the presentation!

🎤 Presentation Rules:
• Must be conducted entirely in English
• Any or all team members can present
• Directly address the judging criteria in your presentation
• Arrive in the virtual waiting room at least 10 minutes early

🏆 The top 10 teams advance to the Grand Finale (Round 3)!

faqs.question: How many teams advance from Round 2 to Round 3? Who evaluates Round 2?
faqs.context: User wants to know the evaluation and how many teams proceed to the final round
faqs.answer:
📊 Round 2 Advancement:

• An expert panel of judges scores all Round 2 presentations
• Judging is based on all 6 criteria (including Prototyping, which is assessed from Round 2 onward)
• The TOP 10 teams with the highest scores advance to the Grand Finale (Round 3)

🎉 Making it to Round 3 means you're among the top 10 teams in Sri Lanka!

faqs.question: What happens in Round 3, the Grand Finale? Format, rules, and what to expect.
faqs.context: User wants to know everything about the final physical hackathon round
faqs.answer:
🔴 Round 3 — Grand Finale (Physical Hackathon)

Date: 12 October 2025
Venue: SLIIT Main Campus, Malabe

This is the ultimate test of your skills, creativity, and teamwork!

⚡ Format:
• A topic or problem statement will be randomly assigned to your team on the day — there's NO prior knowledge of the topic
• Your team has exactly 5 hours to build a working solution from scratch
• No prior preparation for the specific topic is possible — it's pure skill!
• After building, you deliver a 10-minute final presentation to the judges

📦 What to Bring:
• Your laptops and development tools
• Creativity and problem-solving ability!

🏆 After presentations, winners are announced and prizes are awarded on the same day!

💡 Judging in Round 3 includes all 6 criteria, including Prototyping.

faqs.question: Where is Round 3 held? What is the venue for the Grand Finale?
faqs.context: User wants to know the physical location and venue of the final round
faqs.answer:
📍 Grand Finale Venue:

🏛️ Sri Lanka Institute of Information Technology (SLIIT)
Main Campus — Malabe, Sri Lanka

• The event is held IN PERSON at the SLIIT Malabe campus
• All 10 finalist teams must be physically present on 12 October 2025
• Virtual participation is NOT available for Round 3

Make sure your whole team is available and can travel to Malabe on the event day!

faqs.question: What are the competition tracks? What topics can I choose from?
faqs.context: User wants an overview of all available project tracks they can choose from
faqs.answer:
🎯 You must choose your project idea from one of these 6 competition tracks:

1. 🤖 Artificial Intelligence
2. 📡 IoT, Edge & Smart Systems
3. ⛓️ Blockchain & Web3
4. 🌿 Sustainability & Green Tech
5. 🤝 Society, Work & Accessibility
6. 🔬 Emerging Tech Additions

Each track has specific sub-topics. Ask me about a specific track to see all its sub-topics and get guidance on what kinds of projects fit each category!

faqs.question: What are the Artificial Intelligence track topics and sub-topics?
faqs.context: User wants to know what AI-related project ideas are allowed under the AI track
keywords: what can i build in ai track
faqs.answer:
🤖 Track 1: Artificial Intelligence

This track covers 6 sub-topics:

1. Generative AI for Education
   Ideas: AI tutors, content generation, personalized learning platforms

2. AI in Healthcare
   Ideas: Disease detection, patient monitoring, medical image analysis

3. AI in Finance & Cybersecurity
   Ideas: Fraud detection, risk modeling, AI-powered threat detection

4. AI in Creative Industries
   Ideas: AI art tools, music generation, creative assistance platforms

5. Responsible & Ethical AI
   Ideas: Bias detection, AI fairness tools, explainability dashboards

6. AI for Climate & Sustainability
   Ideas: Carbon tracking, environmental monitoring, energy optimization

This is one of the most popular tracks — make sure your solution is genuinely innovative!

faqs.question: What are the IoT, Edge & Smart Systems track topics?
faqs.context: User wants to know about the IoT track, smart systems, and edge computing project ideas
keywords: what can i build in iot track
faqs.answer:
📡 Track 2: IoT, Edge & Smart Systems

This track covers 3 sub-topics:

1. Next-Gen IoT for Smart Cities
   Ideas: Traffic management, smart waste systems, urban monitoring

2. Industrial IoT & Edge Computing
   Ideas: Predictive maintenance, factory automation, edge analytics

3. IoT for Healthcare & Well-being
   Ideas: Remote patient monitoring, wearable health devices, assistive tech

Projects in this track often involve hardware (sensors, microcontrollers) alongside software, but software-only solutions that simulate IoT systems are also acceptable.

faqs.question: What are the Blockchain & Web3 track topics?
faqs.context: User wants to know about blockchain, crypto, and Web3 project ideas in this track
keywords: what can i build in blockchain track
faqs.answer:
⛓️ Track 3: Blockchain & Web3

This track covers 2 sub-topics:

1. Blockchain for Governance & Transparency
   Ideas: Voting systems, supply chain verification, public record management, anti-corruption tools

2. Decentralized Finance & Digital Assets
   Ideas: DeFi platforms, NFT marketplaces, peer-to-peer lending, token economies

Projects should demonstrate a clear understanding of blockchain technology and its real-world application. Smart contracts, decentralization, and immutability are key themes.

faqs.question: What are the Sustainability & Green Tech track topics?
faqs.context: User wants to know about environment, clean energy, and sustainability project ideas
keywords: what can i build in sustainability track
faqs.answer:
🌿 Track 4: Sustainability & Green Tech

This track covers 3 sub-topics:

1. Green Tech & Circular Economy
   Ideas: Waste tracking apps, recycling platforms, sustainable supply chains

2. Renewable Energy & Climate Resilience
   Ideas: Solar energy monitoring, flood prediction, disaster alert systems

3. Agritech & Food Security
   Ideas: Crop disease detection, smart irrigation, food supply optimization

This track is ideal for teams who are passionate about environmental impact and want to build solutions that contribute to a greener, more sustainable future!

faqs.question: What are the Society, Work & Accessibility track topics?
faqs.context: User wants to know about social good, accessibility, and future of work project ideas
keywords: what can i build in society track
faqs.answer:
🤝 Track 5: Society, Work & Accessibility

This track covers 3 sub-topics:

1. Future of Work & Human Augmentation
   Ideas: Productivity tools, AR/VR for remote collaboration, skill development platforms

2. Inclusive Tourism & Culture Tech
   Ideas: Accessible travel apps, cultural heritage preservation, virtual museum experiences

3. Disaster Preparedness & Resilience
   Ideas: Emergency alert systems, community resource apps, post-disaster coordination tools

This track is perfect for teams who want to create technology with meaningful social impact!

faqs.question: What are the Emerging Tech Additions track topics?
faqs.context: User wants to know about futuristic technology tracks like AR/VR, biotech, and brain-computer interfaces
keywords: what can i build in emerging tech track
faqs.answer:
🔬 Track 6: Emerging Tech Additions

This track covers 3 sub-topics:

1. AR/VR & Metaverse Innovations
   Ideas: Immersive education, virtual workspaces, AR navigation, mixed reality games

2. Biotech & Digital Health
   Ideas: Genomics platforms, personal health tracking, mental wellness apps, digital therapeutics

3. Human-Computer Interaction & Brain-Computer Interfaces (BCI)
   Ideas: Gesture-based interfaces, eye-tracking tools, assistive BCI applications

This track is for teams who want to push the boundaries of what's technologically possible. Bold, futuristic ideas are strongly encouraged!

faqs.question: What are the prizes? Cash prizes and awards for winners.
faqs.context: User wants to know about the monetary prizes, certificates, and other rewards for winners and participants
faqs.answer:
🏆 Prize Pool — Mini Hackathon 2026:

💰 Cash Prizes for Top 3 Teams:
• 🥇 1st Place: LKR 30,000
• 🥈 2nd Place: LKR 20,000
• 🥉 3rd Place: LKR 15,000

🎁 All 10 Grand Finale Teams Receive:
• Official printed certificates
• Exclusive MS Club swag and merchandise

🌟 Additional Benefits for All Participants:
• Experience building a real product end-to-end
• Mentorship from industry professionals (Round 2 teams)
• Networking with tech leaders
• Portfolio-worthy project experience
• Recognition from MS Club of SLIIT

Beyond the prizes, Mini Hackathon is a launchpad for your tech career!

faqs.question: How are submissions judged? What are the judging criteria and marking rubric?
faqs.context: User wants to understand exactly how entries are evaluated and scored across all rounds
faqs.answer:
⚖️ Judging Criteria — Mini Hackathon 2026:

All submissions are scored on 6 criteria:

1. 💻 Technology
   Innovative and feasible use of technology. Is the tech stack appropriate and well-implemented?

2. 💡 Innovation
   How original and creative is the idea? Does it address the problem in a new way?

3. 🎯 Concept
   Is the problem clearly defined? Is the target audience well-identified? Is the solution logical?

4. 📈 Feasibility
   Is the solution realistically buildable and commercially viable? Could it scale?

5. 🎤 Presentation
   Is the presentation clear, structured, and persuasive? Do you communicate your idea well?

6. 🖼️ Prototyping (Round 2 & 3 only)
   How well does the prototype/demo demonstrate the core functionality of the solution?

💡 Tips:
• Address each criterion explicitly in your proposal and presentation
• In Round 2 & 3, a strong prototype makes a huge difference
• Clarity and structure in presentations are just as important as the idea itself!

faqs.question: What are the requirements and format for the project proposal?
faqs.context: User wants to know the exact format, length, file type, and rules for writing the project proposal
faqs.answer:
📄 Project Proposal Requirements:

✅ Format & File:
• File types accepted: DOC, DOCX, or PDF
• Must be written in English
• Must be original work (no plagiarism or AI-generated content without disclosure)

📏 Length & Size:
• Maximum 10 pages (excluding table of contents and figures/images)
• File size must be less than 100MB

📝 What to Include:
• Your chosen competition track
• Problem statement and target audience
• Proposed solution and how it works
• Technology stack and tools you'll use
• Feasibility and potential impact
• (Optional for Round 1) Prototype or demo description

⚠️ Note: In Round 2, you'll submit an UPDATED version of this proposal, so make sure your Round 1 document is solid — it forms the foundation!

faqs.question: What are the rules for the Round 1 presentation video?
faqs.context: User wants to know the specific rules, format, and platform for the 3-minute video submission in Round 1
faqs.answer:
🎥 Round 1 Presentation Video Rules:

📋 Strict Requirements:
• Maximum Duration: 3 minutes (going over will disqualify your video)
• Must be created using: PowerPoint or Google Slides (recorded presentation)
• Upload Platform: Microsoft Streams (using your SLIIT email account)
• Voice: Must be your own voice — AI-generated voiceovers are NOT allowed
• Content: Must be original work by your team

✅ Tips for a Great Video:
• Start with your problem statement — hook the judges early
• Clearly explain your solution and how it works
• Mention your track and why your approach is innovative
• End with impact — who benefits and how?
• Practice until you're under 3 minutes comfortably

⚠️ Failure to follow these rules may result in disqualification of your video submission.

faqs.question: What are the rules for the Round 2 live virtual presentation?
faqs.context: User wants to know the rules, format, and expectations for the live presentation in Round 2
faqs.answer:
🎤 Round 2 Live Presentation Rules:

📋 Format:
• Duration: 10-minute presentation + 1-minute Q&A with judges
• Mode: Fully Virtual (online platform — link shared to qualifying teams)
• Language: Must be conducted entirely in English

👥 Who Can Present:
• Any or all team members can participate in the presentation
• Even just one person presenting is perfectly acceptable
• The key is clarity and coverage of the judging criteria

⏰ Timing & Logistics:
• Arrive in the virtual waiting room AT LEAST 10 minutes before your slot
• Late arrival may result in your presentation being skipped
• Your time slot will be communicated after Round 1 results

✅ Content Must Cover:
• Updated solution overview
• Technology and innovation aspects
• Feasibility and market viability
• Your prototype (mandatory in Round 2)
• Directly address each judging criterion

faqs.question: What mentorship is available? How does the mentor program work?
faqs.context: User wants to know about mentors, mentorship program, and how to benefit from the assigned mentor in Round 2
faqs.answer:
🎓 Mentorship Program:

When your team advances to Round 2, you will be assigned a dedicated industry expert mentor!

👤 What Your Mentor Does:
• Provides professional guidance on your project
• Helps you refine your solution and approach
• Offers feedback on your proposal and prototype
• May offer insights on technical choices and market viability

💡 How to Maximize Your Mentorship:
• Come prepared with specific questions
• Share your current proposal and prototype early
• Be open to feedback — mentors have real industry experience
• Use the feedback to significantly improve before your live presentation

📌 Mentor assignment details will be shared directly with your team after Round 1 results are announced.

faqs.question: Do I need to build a prototype? What prototype tools should I use?
faqs.context: User wants to know if a prototype is required, what tools to use, and when it is mandatory vs optional
faqs.answer:
🖼️ Prototype Requirements by Round:

🟡 Round 1:
• Prototype is OPTIONAL but strongly recommended
• Accepted tools: Adobe XD, Figma, or a working software demo
• Including a prototype can meaningfully boost your score

🟠 Round 2:
• Prototype is MANDATORY
• Must be an Adobe XD or Figma UI prototype
• Should demonstrate the core user flows and interface of your solution
• Judges will evaluate this as a separate scoring criterion

🔴 Round 3:
• You will BUILD a working solution from scratch during the 5-hour hackathon
• Whatever you build on the day IS your prototype/deliverable

💡 Tips for Prototypes:
• Focus on the core user journey, not every feature
• Make it clickable/interactive in Figma for best impression
• Ensure it aligns with your proposal's described solution

faqs.question: What can get my team disqualified? Disqualification rules and ineligibility.
faqs.context: User wants to know what actions or conditions would lead to their disqualification
faqs.answer:
⚠️ Disqualification Rules:

Your entry CAN be disqualified if:

❌ Submission Issues:
• Your submission is incomplete, illegible, or fails to meet format requirements
• You submit more than one entry (only the first counts, but repeated violations may disqualify)

❌ Intellectual Property Violations:
• You use another competitor's or person's IP without their explicit written permission
• Your project is substantially copied or plagiarized from existing work

❌ Eligibility Changes:
• A team member drops out of SLIIT or otherwise becomes ineligible DURING the competition
• In this case, you MUST proactively withdraw your team — failure to do so may result in permanent disqualification from future editions

❌ Conduct Violations:
• Harassment of participants, judges, or organizers
• Failure to follow official rules

💡 When in doubt, reach out to info@msclubsliit.org before doing anything that might violate the rules!

faqs.question: What are the code of conduct and behavior rules for participants?
faqs.context: User wants to know about rules of conduct, decisions being final, and expected behavior during the competition
keywords: what are the rules and policies
faqs.answer:
📜 Code of Conduct:

By participating in Mini Hackathon 2026, you agree to:

✅ Rules & Compliance:
• Follow all official Mini Hackathon rules strictly
• Adhere to all submission and conduct requirements

✅ Decisions:
• Accept that the organizers' and judges' decisions are FINAL
• Disputes or appeals against judging decisions are not entertained

✅ Respect & Inclusion:
• Treat all participants, judges, mentors, and organizers with respect
• Zero tolerance for any form of harassment, discrimination, or bullying
• Maintain a positive, inclusive, and collaborative environment

✅ Originality:
• Submit only original work
• Do not use paid assistance, stolen IP, or AI-generated work without disclosure

Violation of any conduct rules may result in immediate disqualification.

faqs.question: Who owns the intellectual property of my project? IP rights and ownership.
faqs.context: User wants to know about IP ownership of their submission and whether they retain rights to their project
keywords: does ms club own my idea
who has rights to my project
can i use my project after
faqs.answer:
💼 Intellectual Property (IP) Rights:

✅ You retain full ownership of your project and ideas.

• Your submission remains YOUR intellectual property
• MS Club of SLIIT does NOT claim ownership over your project
• You are free to continue developing and commercializing your idea after the competition

⚠️ Requirements:
• Your submission must be your OWN original work
• You must have rights to all components, libraries, and assets used
• Using open-source components is fine — just ensure proper licensing
• You cannot submit a project that incorporates another person's or competitor's IP without permission

For specific IP queries, contact info@msclubsliit.org.

faqs.question: What is the Mini Hackathon website? Where can I find more information?
faqs.context: User wants the official website URL, social media links, or where to find official information
faqs.answer:
🌐 Official Mini Hackathon 2026 Links:

• 🔗 Website: minihackathon.msclubsliit.org
• 📧 Email: info@msclubsliit.org

Visit the official website for:
• Registration form
• Official Rules & Regulations document
• Announcements and updates
• Schedule and round results

Follow the MS Club of SLIIT on social media for real-time updates and announcements!

faqs.question: How do I contact the organizers? Who do I reach out to for questions?
faqs.context: User wants to know how to get in touch with the organizing team for questions, issues, or support
faqs.answer:
📬 Contact the Organizers:

📧 Email: info@msclubsliit.org
🌐 Website: minihackathon.msclubsliit.org

Reach out to the MS Club of SLIIT organizing team for:
• Clarification on eligibility questions
• Technical issues with registration or submission
• Questions not covered in the Rules & Regulations
• Reporting errors or problems with your entry

💡 Tips for faster responses:
• Include your team name and team ID in your email
• Be specific about your question or issue
• Reach out early — don't wait until the last day before deadlines!

The team is friendly and happy to help. Don't hesitate to ask! 😊

faqs.question: Can I use AI tools in my project? Are ChatGPT, Copilot, or AI assistants allowed?
faqs.context: User wants to know if using AI coding tools, AI-generated content, or AI assistants is allowed during the competition
faqs.answer:
🤖 AI Tools Policy:

✅ Allowed:
• Using AI tools (like ChatGPT, Copilot) for ASSISTANCE in coding or research
• Incorporating AI/ML as a FEATURE in your project (e.g., building an AI-powered app)
• Using AI to brainstorm or ideate

❌ NOT Allowed:
• AI-generated voiceovers in your Round 1 presentation video — voice must be your own
• Fully AI-generated proposals submitted as original work without any real contribution
• Using paid AI services or hired developers to build your project for you

💡 The key principle is that your submission must represent genuine work by your team. AI can be a tool — not a replacement for your own effort and ideas.

When in doubt, contact info@msclubsliit.org for clarification!

faqs.question: What kind of project should I build? Project ideas and inspiration.
faqs.context: User is looking for inspiration, guidance on what kind of projects are suitable, and what makes a good entry
faqs.answer:
💡 What Makes a Great Mini Hackathon Project?

The best projects typically:

✅ Solve a REAL problem — identify a genuine pain point that real people experience
✅ Are INNOVATIVE — bring a fresh approach, not just another clone of existing apps
✅ Are FEASIBLE — realistic to build within your team's skills and timeframe
✅ Fit a TRACK — strongly aligned with one of the 6 competition tracks
✅ Have IMPACT — clearly explain who benefits and how

🚀 Project Types Accepted:
• Web applications
• Mobile apps (Android/iOS)
• Desktop applications
• IoT systems (software + hardware)
• AI/ML models with interfaces
• Blockchain decentralized apps (DApps)
• AR/VR experiences

📌 Choose a track first, then brainstorm 2–3 project ideas and pick the one that's most original and technically interesting to your team. Ask me about a specific track for targeted project idea inspiration!

faqs.question: Can I change my team members after registration?
faqs.context: User wants to know if they can add, remove, or swap team members after submitting their registration
faqs.answer:
⚠️ Team Composition After Registration:

Generally, team changes after registration are NOT permitted.

• Your team roster is locked at the time of registration
• Adding or removing members after submission is not allowed under normal circumstances

🚨 If Something Happens:
If a team member becomes ineligible (e.g., drops out of SLIIT) after registration:
• You MUST proactively notify and withdraw from the competition
• Failure to declare ineligibility is a conduct violation

📧 For exceptional circumstances, contact info@msclubsliit.org immediately with your team details. The organizers will assess on a case-by-case basis.

faqs.question: Can team members be from different faculties or degree programs?
faqs.context: User wants to know if they can form cross-faculty or cross-program teams
keywords: can people from different programs join
faqs.answer:
✅ Yes! Cross-Faculty and Cross-Program Teams Are Encouraged!

Your team members can be from ANY faculty or degree program at SLIIT, as long as each member:
• Is an actively enrolled SLIIT undergraduate
• Is in 1st, 2nd, or 3rd Year (1st Semester)
• Is at least 16 years old

💡 In fact, we HIGHLY RECOMMEND mixing students from different fields! A team that combines tech, design, and business perspectives tends to produce more well-rounded, innovative, and feasible solutions — and scores better across all judging criteria.

faqs.question: When are results announced? How will I know if I advanced?
faqs.context: User wants to know when and how round results will be communicated to teams
keywords: how will i know if i passed
faqs.answer:
📢 Results Announcements:

• Round 1 Results: Announced after the close of Round 1 (after 30 September 2025)
• Round 2 Results: Announced after Round 2 presentations (after 10 October 2025)

📬 How Will You Be Notified?
• Results are typically communicated via the official website and email
• Follow the official MS Club social media for announcements
• Ensure the email address provided during registration is active and monitored

💡 Keep an eye on:
• minihackathon.msclubsliit.org
• Your registered email inbox
• MS Club of SLIIT's social media pages

faqs.question: How should I prepare for the Mini Hackathon? Tips for success.
faqs.context: User wants advice on how to prepare, improve their chances, and succeed in the competition
faqs.answer:
🚀 Tips to Succeed in Mini Hackathon 2026:

📝 For Round 1:
• Pick a problem you genuinely care about — passion shows in proposals
• Address ALL 5 judging criteria explicitly in your proposal
• Keep your video crisp, engaging, and well within 3 minutes
• Include an optional Figma/XD prototype to stand out

🎤 For Round 2:
• Use your mentor actively — they're an invaluable resource
• Build a polished, interactive Figma prototype
• Practice your 10-minute presentation until it feels natural
• Prepare for Q&A — judges often ask tough but fair questions

⚡ For Round 3:
• Practice rapid prototyping — build small apps quickly under time pressure
• Stay calm under pressure and divide tasks clearly within the team
• Focus on core functionality first, then polish
• Communicate clearly in your final presentation

🌟 General Tips:
• Choose a diverse, complementary team
• Start early — don't leave anything to the last minute
• Solve a REAL problem that you can clearly explain in one sentence
